title: dianthus stramineus
intro: Dianthus stramineus, also known as the straw pink or pink feverfew, is a perennial plant belonging to the carnation family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, headache, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus stramineus include reducing inflammation, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed or applied topically to reap its benefits.
